pattern: A single straight cut splits the grid into a prefix and suffix of rows or columns, so equal halves exist exactly when some prefix sum equals half the total.
hints: A horizontal or vertical cut makes one side a block of leading rows (or columns) and the other the rest.
Both sides are equal only if each is half the grid's total, so an odd total can never work.
Accumulate row sums top-to-bottom and column sums left-to-right; if any running prefix (before the last) hits half the total, return true.

edge_inputs.description: vertical cut works where horizontal fails
balance only at an interior cut

stress.description: random grids with bounded cell count
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    side = max(2, min(size, 300))
    m = rng.randint(1, side)
    n = rng.randint(1, side)
    if m * n < 2:
        n = 2
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
